title: acer insigne var. velutinum
intro: Acer insigne var. velutinum, also known as the downy maple, is a variety of maple tree native to Japan, characterized by its distinctive downy leaves and small stature. The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Acer insigne var. velutinum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and support immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be administered orally to harness its medicinal properties.
